description: Form-driven creation or edit screen with a sticky bottom action bar (Save / Cancel / Discard). Use whenever a designer expects users to fill in fields and commit via primary actions, especially on long forms where the action bar should remain visible during scroll. Common across Actian: glossary term creation, dataset registration, contract authoring, data product editing.

slots.primary: Form fields, sections, validation messages — uses form-create skeleton
slots.footer: Sticky bottom action bar with primary action (Save / Create / Update) and secondary action (Cancel) — uses sticky-footer skeleton

notes: `skeleton.composition: true` signals the renderer to treat the content array as additive on top of the composed recipes' skeletons, rather than as the authoritative skeleton. The composed recipes (form-create + sticky-footer) provide the base structure.

Render the form-create skeleton as the page body. Inject the sticky-footer skeleton as a fixed bottom bar (do not nest it inside the form scroll area). The footer must remain visible regardless of form scroll position.

Footer primary action label mirrors the form intent: 'Create' for new entities, 'Save' for edits, 'Update' for partial updates. Secondary action defaults to 'Cancel' which navigates back without saving.

On mobile/narrow viewports, the footer remains pinned but action labels can shorten (e.g., 'Save' → save icon only). At lo-fi this is a structural decision only — designers can defer label-length decisions to hifi.
examples: Glossary term creation with Save / Cancel footer
Dataset registration with Create / Cancel footer
Data contract authoring with Save Draft / Publish / Cancel footer
Edit user profile with Update / Discard footer
guidance: At hifi, footer should use the elevated surface token (sits above page content with subtle shadow). Primary action stays right-aligned per app-context patterns. Disable the primary action when the form has validation errors. The Cancel action should prompt a confirmation if the form is dirty.
